title: Microsoft Build 2026: Agent Platform, Microsoft IQ, and Governed AI ...
full_text: # Microsoft Build 2026: Agent Platform, Microsoft IQ, and Governed AI for Enterprise. Microsoft used Build 2026 in San Francisco on June 2 to introduce a new Microsoft Agent Platform, seven in-house AI models, expanded Microsoft IQ context services, and developer tools meant to move AI agents from demos into governed enterprise software. It is that the company is trying to make agents feel like the next default computing abstraction inside Microsoft 365, GitHub, Azure, Windows-adjacent devices, and eventually scientific and quantum workflows. The pitch, then, is not “our agent is smarter.” The pitch is “our agent knows where it works, who it works for, what it may touch, and where IT can put a leash on it.” That is less glamorous than a leaderboard score, but it is much closer to the problem administrators and developers face when someone upstairs asks why the company’s expensive AI pilot has not become production software. / score=0.632 / query=AI agent platform general availability enterprise workflow 2026 / intent=find_workflow_change / path=official_original

# Microsoft Build 2026: Agent Platform, Microsoft IQ, and Governed AI for Enterprise.

Microsoft used Build 2026 in San Francisco on June 2 to introduce a new Microsoft Agent Platform, seven in-house AI models, expanded Microsoft IQ context services, and developer tools meant to move AI agents from demos into governed enterprise software.

It is that the company is trying to make agents feel like the next default computing abstraction inside Microsoft 365, GitHub, Azure, Windows-adjacent devices, and eventually scientific and quantum workflows.
The pitch, then, is not “our agent is smarter.
” The pitch is “our agent knows where it works, who it works for, what it may touch, and where IT can put a leash on it.

## clean_text

# Microsoft Build 2026: Agent Platform, Microsoft IQ, and Governed AI for Enterprise. Microsoft used Build 2026 in San Francisco on June 2 to introduce a new Microsoft Agent Platform, seven in-house AI models, expanded Microsoft IQ context services, and developer tools meant to move AI agents from demos into governed enterprise software. It is that the company is trying to make agents feel like the next default computing abstraction inside Microsoft 365, GitHub, Azure, Windows-adjacent devices, and eventually scientific and quantum workflows. The pitch, then, is not “our agent is smarter.” The pitch is “our agent knows where it works, who it works for, what it may touch, and where IT can put a leash on it.” That is less glamorous than a leaderboard score, but it is much closer to the problem administrators and developers face when someone upstairs asks why the company’s expensive AI pilot has not become production software. / score=0.632 / query=AI agent platform general availability enterprise workflow 2026 / intent=find_workflow_change / path=official_original
